opp.name: Rocketlab_Sourcing+EProc+SRM_May26

opp.ais_why:
• Evan Bartholio joined as the Business Systems Engineer driving operations, supply chain, and AI initiatives. An explicit AI mandate from the prospect's own seat.
• Direct customer voice: the team is tired of just looking at reports and wants automation and AI to do the heavy lifting.
• Merlin Intake, autonomous negotiation, and supplier scorecards are all named areas of interest. Next steps scope Merlin Intake, supplier risk, and Teams integration follow-up.

sources: Avoma intro call 04 May 2026, Evan Bartholio
SF Next_Step_History__c: 'point solutions + Infor LN ERP + unnamed RFx platform'
No quantified metrics in Business_Objectives__c, Value_to_Customer__c, or Customer_Business_Problem__c (fields not populated or not present)
metrics.narrative: Evan Bartholio stated on the 04 May intro call that he wanted to 'gather the actual pain points and talk to the people who are living them' before a discovery session, signaling he does not yet have a quantified business case. No cost savings, cycle time reduction, headcount impact, or ROI target has been articulated. The qualitative pain is implied: Rocket Lab uses point solutions plus Infor LN ERP and an unnamed RFx platform (per Next_Step_History__c and 04 May call context), suggesting fragmented sourcing/procurement processes. However, no procurement stakeholder has quantified the operational cost of that fragmentation—error rates, manual effort, supplier risk exposure, or spend under management gaps. The highest-leverage move is to run a discovery call with the procurement and supply chain ops stakeholders Evan mentioned, quantify the pain (annual spend managed manually, cycle time for RFx rounds, supplier risk incidents), and build a defensible ROI case tied to the relevant stakeholder growth and government contract compliance burden.

sources: Avoma intro call 04 May: Evan Bartholio engaged, requested materials, stated intent to gather internal pain points
No follow-up call or buyer reply in 55 days (last activity 05 May, today 29 Jun)
SF Next Step 22 Jun: rep reached out, no buyer response
No other stakeholder engaged, no introductions made, no advocacy evidence
No Champion contact role in Salesforce
champion.narrative: No champion. Evan Bartholio is a technical evaluator and internal mobilizer, not an advocate. He engaged on one intro call (04 May), requested materials to inform internal discovery, and then went silent for 55 days. He has not introduced other stakeholders, has not advocated for Zycus, and has not replied to the rep's 22 Jun follow-up. His 04 May request to 'gather pain points internally' suggests he intended to build a case, but his silence since then signals he either failed to mobilize stakeholders, lost internal priority, or lacks the political capital to drive this initiative. Israel Powell (Manager, Supply Chain Operations) has never been engaged and is not a champion. No Economic Buyer or executive sponsor is in the stakeholder map. A champion requires access to the EB, active advocacy (introductions, pushing the agenda, defending Zycus against competitors), and current engagement—none of which exist here. The move is to re-engage Evan with a direct ask ('What happened after our 04 May call? Did you gather the internal pain points? Can we schedule the discovery call this week?'), and if he remains unresponsive, escalate to his manager or Israel Powell to restart the conversation and identify who can champion this internally.

sources: Next_Step_History__c: 'point solutions + Infor LN ERP + unnamed RFx platform'
Avoma intro call 04 May: Evan described current stack, no named competitor mentioned
No Competitors__c, Others_Competitors_Please_specify__c, or competitive mention in tasks/next steps
Competitor field in this sweep: 'Status Quo / Do Nothing (current point solutions + Infor LN ERP + unnamed RFx platform)'
competition.narrative: The primary competitor is Status Quo / Do Nothing. Rocket Lab's current stack is 'point solutions plus Infor LN ERP plus an unnamed RFx platform' (per Next_Step_History__c and 04 May call context). No named competitor (Coupa, SAP Ariba, GEP, Jaggaer) has been mentioned in calls, tasks, or Salesforce fields. Evan has not stated they are running a competitive RFP or benchmarking other vendors. The risk is that Status Quo wins by inertia: if Evan cannot mobilize internal stakeholders or quantify the pain of fragmented systems, the evaluation stalls and no decision is made. The wedge against Status Quo is to quantify the cost of the current fragmented approach—manual effort, lack of supplier risk visibility, slow sourcing cycles, Govcloud compliance burden—and position Zycus as the unified platform that eliminates those costs while enabling AI-driven efficiency (Merlin Intake for orchestration, Merlin negotiation agent for cost savings). The activation is to run a discovery call with the pain owners (Israel Powell, procurement lead), quantify the pain, and build a compelling ROI case that makes doing nothing more expensive than buying Zycus.

sources: Avoma intro call 04 May: Evan described 'point solutions + Infor LN ERP + unnamed RFx platform', stated need to gather pain points from 'people living them'
SF OpportunityContactRole: Israel Powell, Manager Supply Chain Operations (pain owner, not yet engaged)
Next_Step_History__c: references fragmented stack, no quantified impact
No Customer_Business_Problem__c, Pain_points_in_the_current_solution__c, or Value_to_Customer__c content
identify_pain.narrative: Pain is articulated qualitatively but not quantified, and the pain owner is not yet engaged. Evan Bartholio described Rocket Lab's current state as 'point solutions plus Infor LN ERP plus an unnamed RFx platform' (per Next_Step_History__c context and 04 May call), implying fragmented sourcing and procurement systems with no unified supplier risk or intake orchestration. He stated he needed to 'gather the actual pain points and talk to the people who are living them'—the procurement and supply chain ops teams—before a discovery call, signaling he does not own the pain himself. Israel Powell (Manager, Supply Chain Operations, on contact roles) is the likely pain owner but has not been engaged on a call. No operational impact has been quantified: no cycle time for RFx rounds, no manual effort (FTE cost), no supplier risk incidents, no spend visibility gaps. The Govcloud compliance requirement (US government contracts) adds regulatory pressure, but the cost of non-compliance or the burden of manual compliance checks has not been stated. The move is to insist on a discovery call with Israel and the procurement lead(s) Evan mentioned, and on that call quantify the pain—annual contract volume, sourcing cycle time, supplier risk exposure, and the cost of fragmented systems.

sources: Avoma intro call 04 May: Evan asked about Govcloud functional limitations, Steve committed to follow up, no response provided (per Next_Step_History__c and commitments)
No MSA, SOW, legal, or InfoSec discussion in call history or Salesforce
No Current_Contract_Expiration__c populated
No SI/partner mentioned
paper_process.narrative: Paper process and contracting mechanics are entirely unknown. No discussion of MSA, SOW, legal review, or procurement channel has occurred. Evan raised a Govcloud compliance question on 04 May ('do other Govcloud customers face functional limitations?'), signaling that Rocket Lab's US government contract obligations may impose InfoSec, data residency, or deployment restrictions, but Steve committed to follow up and no answer has been provided. If Zycus cannot support Govcloud or requires a custom deployment, that is a blocking issue that must be resolved before any commercial conversation. No SI/partner involvement is documented, and no Current_Contract_Expiration__c date is populated (no incumbent contract forcing function). The move is to (1) immediately confirm Zycus Govcloud capability and any limitations, and (2) once the EB is engaged, map the contracting and InfoSec approval process—who reviews SaaS agreements, what the InfoSec checklist is, and whether procurement runs this or if it is IT-led.

sources: Salesforce OpportunityContactRole: Evan Bartholio, Business Systems Manager; Israel Powell, Manager Supply Chain Operations
Avoma intro call 04 May: Evan deferred budget/contract questions, stated need to gather internal pain points first
No exec-level contact role (no VP, CFO, or C-level) in Salesforce
No EB/Executive Sponsor boolean or custom field populated
economic_buyer.narrative: No Economic Buyer identified or engaged. Evan Bartholio, Business Systems Manager (mid-level IT role per Salesforce Contact), deferred budget and contract authority questions on the 04 May call, stating he first needed to 'gather pain points internally' before involving decision-makers. For a $150k multi-year SaaS platform purchase at an aerospace/defense contractor with government compliance requirements (Govcloud), budget authority sits with the relevant stakeholder, CFO, or VP Operations—none are in the stakeholder map or call history. Israel Powell (Manager, Supply Chain Operations, also on contact roles) has not been engaged on a call. Without EB engagement, no budget discussion has occurred, no approval process is mapped, and the deal cannot advance beyond technical exploration. The evidence shows Evan is a technical evaluator and internal mobilizer, not a buyer. The move is to ask Evan directly (or his manager if he remains unresponsive) who controls the FY27 procurement technology budget and to request an introduction, framing this as essential to scope ROI and timeline.

sources: Avoma intro call 04 May: Evan described need for internal discovery, no process steps confirmed
SF Next Step 22 Jun: 'pre-demo call in June' implies discovery → demo sequence, not confirmed by buyer
No multi-stakeholder calls, no exec escalation visible in call history
No What_is_the_decision_process__c, Mandate__c, or Purchase_approvals_Required_from__c content
decision_process.narrative: Decision process is unmapped. The call history shows only one intro call (04 May, Evan Bartholio), so no multi-stage buyer process is visible. Evan stated he needed to 'gather pain points and talk to the people living them' before a discovery call, implying an internal discovery phase, but he has not reported back. No procurement, legal, InfoSec, or executive stakeholders have joined, so the approval chain is unknown. The Next Step 22 Jun mentions 'follow up for a pre-demo call in June', suggesting the intended sequence is internal discovery → pre-demo discovery call → demo → (presumably) RFP or vendor selection, but none of that has been confirmed by the buyer. Rocket Lab is a US aerospace/defense company with government contracts (Govcloud requirement), so the process likely includes InfoSec review, legal (MSA/SOW), procurement, and executive sign-off, but none of these gates are documented. The move is to ask Evan (or escalate if he remains unresponsive) what the approval process looks like: who needs to see a demo, who approves budget, what the timeline is, and whether this is a sole-source evaluation or a competitive RFP.

sources: Avoma intro call 04 May: Evan requested materials to inform internal discovery, no criteria shared
SF Next Step 22 Jun: 'pre-demo call in June' planned but not scheduled
No RFP, RFI, or Business_Requirements__c content in Salesforce
Products__c populated (iSource, iSupplier, eProcurement, Merlin, Merlin Intake, Agentic AI, ANA) but no confirmed buyer prioritization
decision_criteria.narrative: Decision criteria are unknown. No RFP, RFI, scorecard, or structured evaluation has been shared. Evan requested materials on iSource (Sourcing), Supplier Risk & Performance Management, and Merlin Intake on 04 May to inform internal discovery, but he has not replied with findings or next steps. The product scope (iSource, iSupplier, eProcurement, Merlin suite per SF Products__c) implies criteria around sourcing automation, supplier risk visibility, intake/orchestration, and AI-driven negotiation, but no buyer stakeholder has confirmed weightings, must-haves, or evaluation timeline. The 22 Jun Next Step references a 'pre-demo call in June' (now overdue), which would typically surface criteria, but that call has not been scheduled. The move is to re-engage Evan (or escalate to his manager or Israel Powell) and ask what the evaluation process and decision criteria are—if they are running an RFP, if they have a scorecard, and what functional and compliance requirements (especially Govcloud) are non-negotiable.

For a $150k SaaS platform buy, executive sponsorship (VP Supply Chain, CFO) is mandatory. Without EB engagement, deal will not advance beyond exploration.

Close date 24 Sep 2026 (90 days out) is not credible given current state: still in Qualified stage after 57 days, only one intro call completed 8 weeks ago, no follow-up scheduled, no discovery depth, no EB engaged, no RFP/demo/POC planned. Normal enterprise motion from Qualified to Closed-Won takes 12-15 months; this deal would need to accelerate dramatically in next 2-3 weeks to make 24 Sep feasible.

items.action: Executive connect: the deal owner's manager to call Evan Bartholio's manager or Israel Powell (Manager, Supply Chain Operations) directly to restart the conversation. Frame it as 'We introduced Zycus to Evan in early May, he requested materials to gather internal pain points, and we have not heard back. Can you help us understand if this is still a priority for Rocket Lab and who we should be talking to about sourcing/procurement platform modernization?' Goal is to bypass the stall, map the real decision-makers (VP Supply Chain, CFO), and either restart with executive sponsorship or disqualify.
items.trigger: 55-day buyer silence after Evan requested materials and stated intent to gather internal pain points on 04 May call. Rep follow-up 22 Jun, no reply.
items.expected_effect: Restarts engagement at higher level, maps Economic Buyer and real decision-makers, confirms whether this is a live FY27 priority or a dead exploration. Without this, deal stays dark.

items.action: Deal team: immediately confirm Zycus Govcloud capability and any functional limitations or deployment restrictions, and send written answer to Evan (and cc his manager if escalation happens). This was committed 04 May and is 55 days overdue. If Zycus cannot support Govcloud or requires expensive workaround, disqualify now rather than investing further. If Govcloud is supported, this removes a blocking objection and opens the commercial path.
items.trigger: Evan asked 04 May whether other Govcloud customers face limitations. Steve committed to follow up, no response provided.
items.expected_effect: Resolves potential blocker (US government contract compliance requirement). If supportable, clears path forward; if not, saves time by disqualifying early.

items.action: Deal team: if rank-1 executive connect succeeds in restarting conversation, insist on a discovery call with the pain owners—Israel Powell (Manager, Supply Chain Operations) and the procurement lead(s)—within 7 days. Agenda: quantify the operational cost of the current fragmented stack (annual contract volume, sourcing cycle time, manual effort FTE cost, supplier risk incidents, Govcloud compliance burden), map the evaluation process and approval chain, and identify the Economic Buyer. Do not accept another delay or 'we will get back to you' without a committed next meeting.
items.trigger: Evan stated 04 May he needed to 'gather pain points from the people living them' before discovery call, but has not done so in 55 days. No quantified business case exists.
items.expected_effect: Builds the quantified ROI case needed to justify $150k spend, engages the real pain owners, maps the EB and approval process, and moves deal from exploration to active evaluation. Without this, no commercial conversation is possible.

items.action: Deal team: on the discovery call (rank 3), position Merlin AI (Merlin Intake orchestration + Merlin negotiation agent on supplier pricing) as the ROI centerpiece. Frame it as 'Here is how three aerospace/defense customers quantified 18-25% cycle time reduction and 8-12% supplier cost savings in year one with Merlin—let us model that for Rocket Lab.' Make AI the wedge against Status Quo inertia, not just a feature add-on.
items.trigger: Merlin, Merlin Intake, Agentic AI are in product scope (SF Products__c) and Evan requested Merlin Intake materials 04 May, but no AI discovery depth. AIS not populated, AI ROI not quantified.
items.expected_effect: Differentiates Zycus from the relevant stakeholder and unnamed competitors by quantifying AI-driven ROI. Moves AI from 'nice to have' to 'business case driver' and positions Merlin as the reason to act now vs stay with fragmented point solutions.

items.action: Deal team: if Evan or his manager confirms this is not a live FY27 priority or if re-engagement fails after two attempts (rank 1 + one follow-up), disqualify and close Lost to 'No Decision / Timing'. Do not let this sit in Pipeline consuming forecast capacity. Capture the lesson: mid-level single-threaded contact with no exec sponsorship and no quantified pain = exploration, not a deal.
items.trigger: 60 days in Qualified, 55-day buyer silence, close date 87 days out with zero commercial progress, no EB engaged, no discovery call scheduled.
items.expected_effect: Cleans forecast, frees capacity to focus on winnable deals, and captures honest read that this was never a qualified opportunity without EB sponsorship and quantified business case.

No additional calls, no stakeholder introductions, no EB engagement, no forward progress. Evan may have hit internal blocker (could not mobilize stakeholders, competing priority, or lack of political capital). Deal requires immediate re-engagement and escalation.

north_star_verdict.math: Created 30 Apr, 60 days in Qualified stage as of 29 Jun. No qualified_date or stage-progression submission dates available. Close date 24 Sep (87 days out) requires transition through Formal Eval, Shortlisted, Vendor Selected, Negotiation, and Contract stages in under 12 weeks—enterprise motion typically 12-15 months. No forward slip recorded but original close date is not credible. Last buyer touch 05 May (55 days ago); rep outreach 22 Jun (7 days ago) awaiting reply. Two forcing conditions active: COLD (60+ day silence threshold not yet breached but 55-day gap with cooling engagement qualifies as off-track) and single-threaded mid-level contact with no EB. Forecast Pipeline is appropriate but deal requires immediate executive engagement to restart.

north_star_verdict.headline: Deal is cold: 55 days since last buyer engagement (05 May), no response to rep outreach dated 22 Jun, and close date 87 days away with zero commercial progress. Evan Bartholio has not replied to materials or follow-up after requesting them 04 May to 'gather actual pain points internally'. Without Economic Buyer engagement, no discovery call scheduled, and a stalled internal evaluation, this deal cannot progress to close by 24 Sep 2026.

flags: Single-thread risk: only one active buyer contact (Evan Bartholio, Business Systems Manager) engaged; Israel Powell (Manager, Supply Chain Ops) attended 04 May call but was mostly silent and has not re-engaged. No VP, director, or executive on this deal. Multi-thread to the relevant stakeholder (Cameron McCarter, account contact list) or procurement leadership to test whether this is a real strategic priority or a dead end.

No Economic Buyer identified or engaged: Evan deferred budget and contract questions on 04 May, confirming he is a technical evaluator with no commercial authority. No CFO, VP Finance, VP Supply Chain, or executive sponsor has appeared. For a $150k+ SaaS platform buy, EB sponsorship is mandatory to advance beyond exploration. Next move: ask Evan who controls procurement software budget and secure intro to that person (likely VP Supply Chain Cameron McCarter or finance leadership per account contact list), or if Evan remains unresponsive, reach out directly to the relevant stakeholder to introduce Zycus and test executive appetite.

Single-threaded: only Evan Bartholio (Business Systems Manager, mid-level) engaged on one call 04 May, no response in 55 days. Israel Powell (Manager, Supply Chain Operations) and procurement stakeholders not engaged. No exec sponsor.
No Economic Buyer identified or engaged. For $150k SaaS platform buy at aerospace/defense contractor, VP Supply Chain or CFO approval is mandatory. Evan deferred budget/contract questions.
No quantified business case or ROI target. Evan stated need to gather pain points internally but has not reported findings. No cost savings, cycle time, or operational impact quantified.

Govcloud compliance blocker unresolved: Evan asked about functional limitations 04 May, Steve committed to follow up, no response provided 55 days later. May block deal if Zycus cannot support Govcloud.
best_practice_check.summary: Critical gaps: single-threaded (only Evan engaged, mid-level, unresponsive 55 days), no Economic Buyer or executive sponsor, no discovery depth, no champion, no quantified business case, Govcloud blocker unresolved, close date not credible. Deal is cooling and requires immediate executive-level re-engagement.

Wedge is to quantify cost of fragmentation and position Zycus unified platform + Merlin AI as ROI-positive vs doing nothing.

• Merlin Intake, autonomous negotiation, and supplier scorecards are all named areas of interest. Next steps scope Merlin Intake, supplier risk, and Teams integration follow-up.

deltas.subject: Champion at risk of fading: Evan expressed genuine interest and took ownership of next steps on 04 May but has not replied in 52 days despite rep outreach 22 Jun. His silence suggests either internal discovery stalled, priority dropped, or he lacks influence to mobilize stakeholders. Re-engage with direct ask about internal progress and stakeholder expansion; if no reply in 5 business days, escalate outreach to his manager or VP Supply Chain to bypass potential blocker.

Close date 24 Sep 2026 (87 days out) not credible: 60 days in Qualified, no discovery call, no demo, no RFP, no EB engagement. Enterprise motion typically 12-15 months; this would require 12-week sprint with no current momentum.

Close date 24 Sep 2026 (90 days out) is not credible given current state: still in Qualified stage after 57 days, only one intro call completed 8 weeks ago, no follow-up scheduled, no discovery depth, no EB engaged, no RFP/demo/POC planned. Normal enterprise motion from Qualified to Closed-Won takes 12-15 months; this deal would need to accelerate dramatically in next 2-3 weeks to make 24 Sep feasible.

Single-thread risk: only one active buyer contact (Evan Bartholio, Business Systems Manager) engaged; Israel Powell (Manager, Supply Chain Ops) attended 04 May call but was mostly silent and has not re-engaged. No VP, director, or executive on this deal. Multi-thread to the relevant stakeholder (Cameron McCarter, account contact list) or procurement leadership to test whether this is a real strategic priority or a dead end.

Champion at risk of fading: Evan expressed genuine interest and took ownership of next steps on 04 May but has not replied in 52 days despite rep outreach 22 Jun. His silence suggests either internal discovery stalled, priority dropped, or he lacks influence to mobilize stakeholders. Re-engage with direct ask about internal progress and stakeholder expansion; if no reply in 5 business days, escalate outreach to his manager or VP Supply Chain to bypass potential blocker.

No Economic Buyer identified or engaged: Evan deferred budget and contract questions on 04 May, confirming he is a technical evaluator with no commercial authority. No CFO, VP Finance, VP Supply Chain, or executive sponsor has appeared. For a $150k+ SaaS platform buy, EB sponsorship is mandatory to advance beyond exploration. Next move: ask Evan who controls procurement software budget and secure intro to that person (likely VP Supply Chain Cameron McCarter or finance leadership per account contact list), or if Evan remains unresponsive, reach out directly to the relevant stakeholder to introduce Zycus and test executive appetite.

packets.subject: Cameron McCarter

value.name: Status Quo / Do Nothing (current point solutions + Infor LN ERP + unnamed RFx platform)
value.quote: We use some other third party systems to help manage quoting, and I think some of the interest we have is learning about your agentic features and simplifying the processes that we have and leveraging more automation. We've been doing a lot of reporting on our own with data, tired of just looking at reports and looking at data, just let's have some automations and see if AI can do any of the heavy lifting. (Evan Bartholio, 04 May 2026)
value.source: Avoma call 04 May 2026, Evan Bartholio
value.how_we_win: Quantify the cost of manual supplier risk monitoring and reporting overhead (FTE hours, supplier disruption incidents, lack of proactive geopolitical/financial risk visibility). Position Zycus as the AI-native consolidation that replaces fragmented point solutions, integrates with Infor LN, and automates the pain Evan owns. Anchor the Ana autonomous negotiation agent and supplier risk AI as the differentiated capabilities unavailable in current stack. Secure VP Supply Chain or CFO sponsorship to overcome inertia and fund the business case.

value.how_we_win: Quantify the cost of fragmented systems (manual effort, lack of supplier risk visibility, slow sourcing cycles, Govcloud compliance burden) and position Zycus unified platform + Merlin AI (Intake orchestration, negotiation agent) as ROI-positive. Run discovery with pain owners (Israel Powell, procurement) to build compelling business case that makes doing nothing more expensive than buying.

packets.subject: Status Quo / Do Nothing

value.detail: Close date 24 Sep 2026 (87 days out) is not credible given current state: still in Qualified stage after 60 days, only one intro call completed 55 days ago, no follow-up scheduled, no discovery depth, no EB engaged, no RFP/demo/POC planned. Normal enterprise motion from Qualified to Closed-Won takes 12-15 months; this deal would need to accelerate dramatically in next 2-3 weeks to make 24 Sep feasible.
value.source: SF CreatedDate 30 Apr, Stage Qualified 60 days, CloseDate 24 Sep, no progression
